meta.name: Chicken Tikka Masala
meta.subtitle: Yogurt-marinated chicken with a smoky tikka sear in a creamy tomato masala sauce

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/tikka_masala.json
notes: Weeknight-friendly version using broiler/grill pan or oven; supports stovetop-only fallback.
Deterministic quantities and timing for consistent results across home kitchens.

summary: Aromatic chicken tikka seared hot for char, then simmered in a creamy tomato-onion masala with garam masala and a bright finish.

yield.notes: Serves 4–6 depending on sides. Ideal with basmati rice or naan.

notes: Contains dairy (yogurt/cream/butter). For dairy-free, use coconut yogurt + coconut cream + oil.

optional.label: Grill pan
optional.notes: Great for char if you don't want to use the broiler.

ingredients.label: Chicken thighs, boneless
notes: Thighs stay juicy under high heat.
You can use breast, but reduce sear time and simmer gently.

ingredients.label: Plain yogurt (full-fat preferred)
notes: Whisk smooth for even coating; avoid sweetened yogurt.

ingredients.label: Kashmiri chili powder (or mild chili powder)
notes: For more heat, add 1/4–1/2 tsp cayenne.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Split between marinade, sauce, and final adjustment.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
notes: For the tikka sear; use avocado/canola/grapeseed.

ingredients.label: Butter
notes: Use ghee for a more Indian-forward profile; oil for dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Crushed tomatoes
notes: One standard can (14–15 oz).

ingredients.label: Smoked paprika (optional but helps tikka-style smokiness)
Heavy cream
notes: Use coconut cream for dairy-free.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Adjust to desired sauce thickness.

ingredients.label: Kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
notes: Classic restaurant aroma; optional but strongly recommended.

steps.title: Marinate the chicken
instructions: In a bowl, whisk yogurt, ginger, garlic, cumin, coriander, turmeric, Kashmiri chili, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1.5 tsp salt, and 1 tbsp oil.
Add chicken and toss until evenly coated.
Marinate 20–30 minutes at room temp (or up to 24 hours refrigerated).
checks: Marinade should coat chicken without being watery.
If marinating overnight, bring chicken closer to room temp before broiling for better char.

steps.title: Cook chicken tikka (broiler or skillet)
instructions: Heat broiler to high. Line a sheet pan with foil and place a wire rack on top (recommended for airflow).
Arrange chicken pieces in a single layer. Broil 6–8 minutes, then flip and broil 4–6 minutes until charred at edges and mostly cooked.
Alternative (stovetop): Sear chicken in a hot skillet with 1 tbsp oil, 3–4 minutes per side until browned; it will finish in sauce.
checks: Aim for char at edges; do not fully dry out chicken.
If using breast meat, pull earlier to avoid toughness.

steps.title: Build the tomato-onion masala sauce
instructions: In a large skillet or pot, melt butter over medium heat.
Add diced onion with a pinch of salt; cook 6–8 minutes until soft and lightly golden.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute to toast.
Add crushed tomatoes, remaining 1.5 tsp salt (start with 1 tsp, adjust later), cumin, coriander, smoked paprika (optional), and 1/2 cup water.
Simmer 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and tastes less raw.
checks: Sauce should bubble gently, not splatter violently.
If sauce tastes sharp, simmer a few minutes longer.

steps.title: Blend sauce for a restaurant-smooth texture (optional)
instructions: Optional: Blend sauce until smooth (immersion blender or countertop blender).
Return sauce to pan and bring back to a gentle simmer.
checks: If using countertop blender, vent lid to avoid steam pressure.

steps.title: Finish with cream and chicken tikka
instructions: Lower heat to medium-low. Stir in cream and garam masala.
Add broiled/seared chicken and any collected juices.
Simmer 8–12 minutes until chicken is cooked through and sauce is cohesive.
Stir in remaining 1 tbsp lemon juice (to taste) and crushed kasuri methi (optional).
checks: Do not boil hard after adding cream—gentle simmer prevents splitting.
Chicken should be fully cooked (165°F internal at thickest point).

steps.title: Garnish and serve
instructions: Taste and adjust: add salt if flat, lemon if dull, or a pinch of sugar if too acidic.
Garnish with cilantro and mint if using.
Serve hot with basmati rice, naan, or roti.
checks: Balance should be: savory, aromatic, lightly tangy, creamy.

notes: A cooling yogurt side balances spice and richness.
If serving with rice, keep sauce slightly looser.
Cool quickly and refrigerate in a shallow container.
Reheat gently; add a splash of water to loosen sauce.
Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight in fridge.
Reheat gently to avoid sauce splitting.
Chicken must reach safe internal temperature before serving.
Avoid hard boiling once dairy is added to prevent curdling/splitting.
Cut into larger chunks to reduce drying.
Broil 4–5 minutes per side; finish quickly in sauce.
Use coconut yogurt in marinade and coconut cream in sauce.
Replace butter with neutral oil or coconut oil.
Add 1/2 tsp cayenne and 1 sliced green chili to the sauce.
Finish with extra lemon to keep the heat bright.
Weeknight-friendly if chicken is pre-marinated.
Excellent for batch cooking; flavors deepen overnight.
